summary.intent: Fix all open PR review comments on PR #408 (moltis-org/moltis), merge main into the branch, and keep the branch up to date with remote.
summary.outcome: Successfully merged main twice, fixed glslc→glslang-tools→LunarG Vulkan SDK CI package issue, deduplicated meminfo helpers, fixed gguf_backend_description GPU fallback, added BackendAlreadyInitialized debug log, updated docs, committed and pushed. A large backend concurrency refactor was scoped out as a follow-up.

repo: sandbox.rs was refactored into a sandbox/ module directory on main; branches that touched it see a delete conflict during merge.
GgufRuntimeDevice lives in a pub(crate) module (local_gguf::runtime_devices), so it cannot be constructed in tests from the gateway crate without making the module pub.
LlamaBackend from llama-cpp-2 is an empty struct used purely as a proof-of-initialization token; load_from_file takes &LlamaBackend but never reads from it.
glslc is NOT available as an apt package on Ubuntu 22.04 (Jammy); glslang-tools also does NOT ship glslc — the LunarG Vulkan SDK is the correct source.
The --all-features Rust CI job only runs on main or when GPU-related files change, so Vulkan build errors can be missed in PRs.
local-validate.sh checks SHA equality between local HEAD and PR head on GitHub; a forced push or history rewrite on either side causes it to refuse to run.
The 4 sandboxes.spec.js E2E failures (looking for a Refresh button) are pre-existing flakes requiring Docker, unrelated to provider/CI changes.

code.finding: read_proc_meminfo and parse_meminfo_kb were duplicated between local_gguf/system_info.rs and local_llm/system_info.rs; canonical location is runtime_devices.rs as pub(crate).

gguf_backend_description and gguf_backend_note returned CPU-inference strings for unknown GPU backends (ROCm, HIP, SYCL) even when has_gpu() was true; fixed by adding a None guard for the unknown-backend+GPU case.
Making runtime_devices pub (instead of pub(crate)) is needed to allow gateway-crate tests to construct GgufRuntimeDevice for unit testing backend description logic.
Vulkan shader compilation in CI requires installing glslc from the LunarG Vulkan SDK apt repo, not from Ubuntu's glslang-tools package.

workflow: git stash + pop can silently drop uncommitted changes when a hook or external process modifies files during the stash operation — prefer explicit branch commits or patches over stashing during active editing sessions.
greptile automated review comments can contradict human reviewer comments and each other; verify package contents independently (e.g., packages.ubuntu.com) before acting on either.
OnceLock::get_or_try_init is unstable in stable Rust; use get_or_init with a panic on unexpected error, or OnceLock<Option<T>>, as a workaround for fallible one-time initialization.
Running cargo fmt before committing is required; the repo enforces formatting and the local-validate.sh script checks it.

friction: git stash + pop dropped all uncommitted changes mid-session, forcing a full re-application of all edits from scratch.
local-validate.sh refused to run because local HEAD SHA didn't match PR head SHA after a history divergence, requiring a git reset --hard to remote.
GgufRuntimeDevice being in a pub(crate) module blocked writing gateway-crate unit tests until the module visibility was widened.
OnceLock::get_or_try_init being unstable required a workaround (OnceLock<Option<T>>) for the shared backend initialization pattern.
Conflicting PR review comments (greptile vs human reviewer) about glslc vs glslang-tools required independent research to resolve correctly.
Docker unavailability on the local machine prevented direct verification of Ubuntu package contents, requiring web search.
open_items: Backend concurrency race between detect_runtime_support() and model loads (comments #4/#5): requires a shared OnceLock<LlamaBackend> so LlamaBackend is initialized once and reused rather than init/drop per call. Intentionally deferred as a larger architectural change.
The --all-features CI job is skipped for PRs unless GPU-related files change, meaning Vulkan build errors can go undetected until merge to main.
Sandbox E2E tests (sandboxes.spec.js) have 4 pre-existing flakes related to a missing Refresh button in Docker container UI; root cause not investigated.
